contribution.title: 1919 eclipse expedition
contribution.description: Measured the deflection of starlight by the Sun, confirming Einstein's GR prediction.

contribution.title: Eddington-Finkelstein coordinates
contribution.description: Coordinate system that removes the coordinate singularity at the Schwarzschild radius.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Useful for any module that needs to integrate geodesics across the event horizon.

contribution.title: Eddington limit
contribution.description: Upper limit on the luminosity of a stable, spherically symmetric accreting object.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Defines the natural scale for accretion-rate parameters in RUNS.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Combine careful observation, physical reasoning, and clean coordinate choices.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What coordinate system makes this physics most transparent?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Initially resisted Chandrasekhar's white-dwarf mass limit; let prior beliefs slow acceptance.

reference.citation: Dyson, F. W., Eddington, A. S., Davidson, C. (1920). A determination of the deflection of light by the Sun's gravitational field. Phil. Trans. Roy. Soc. A 220, 291.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Working with horizon-crossing geodesics, accretion-rate normalization, or gravitational lensing observables.
how_to_apply.cautions: Eddington-limit accretion-rate constraints are approximations; real systems can exceed it in beamed or transient regimes.
